hard.opp_name: Dana Farber Cancer Institute - eProc/CLM/ANA - April'26
hard.next_step: Jun 19, 2026: BDR reengaging to discuss Workday go-live progress. Key contacts: Ronnie Crecco (Senior Purchasing Specialist), Michael Katzeff (Sr. Manager, Procurement Operations), Charles Maranga (Procurement Specialist). Interest: AI-driven procurement automation for pharmaceutical procurement and $1B+ hospital construction.
hard.ais_why: October discovery noted strong interest for AI-driven automation across sourcing, contract management, eProcurement and invoicing, with ANNA negotiation and Contract Intelligence as named asks. Workday go-live in January is the gating event; the team explicitly deferred any Zycus evaluation until post-stabilization, so interest is real but currently parked. Only one substantive call plus a short Feb follow-up; a SharePoint resource site is the holding pattern.

north_star_verdict.headline: Discovery-stage deal dark for ~8 months. The single contact, Michael Katzeff (Influencer, no budget authority), has not replied to two BDR re-engagement attempts (18 Mar, 19 Jun 2026) even though the Workday go-live blocker cleared in January. No economic buyer, no champion, no multi-threading, no evaluation launched.
north_star_verdict.math: Today 6 Jul 2026; close 2027-01-01, days_to_close 179. In Qualified since creation 31 Oct 2025 (~248 days), zero stage progression; no Formal Evaluation / Shortlisted / Vendor Selected submission dates exist. Last verified buyer engagement was the 31 Oct 2025 discovery call (~248 days ago); the 17 Feb 2026 follow-up has no transcript and last_activity_date is still 31 Oct 2025. Buyer deferred all next steps to after the January Workday go-live; that event has passed for ~5 months with no reactivation. Time-in-stage is roughly 3x the healthy 60-90 day window for early discovery. To close by 1 Jan 2027 the buyer would need to reactivate, name an economic buyer, and reach Formal Evaluation within ~60 days; on current pace the date is not credible and will slip.
evidence: Last verified buyer engagement was the 31 Oct 2025 discovery call with Michael Katzeff (Influencer) and Charles Maranga (Procurement Specialist); no buyer reply since (Avoma 31 Oct 2025; last_activity_date 2025-10-31).
Buyer explicitly deferred any evaluation until after the January Workday go-live: Michael Katzeff said 'I don't think that we would move forward before then' (Avoma 31 Oct 2025).
Workday went live in January 2026 and has been live ~5 months, but the buyer has not reactivated despite two BDR attempts on 18 Mar 2026 and 19 Jun 2026 (SF Next_Step__c).

No economic buyer engaged. Katzeff said approval requires looping in higher-ups, the Contracting Director (Susan Wills), Legal, Finance/AP and potentially the CFO or board, but none have been contacted (Avoma 31 Oct 2025).
Single-threaded on Michael Katzeff, the only Salesforce contact role (Influencer, no budget authority), now unresponsive for ~8 months.
No champion, no Decision Maker contact role, no executive sponsor on the account.
No stage progression in ~248 days (Qualified since creation); close date already slipped from 2026-05-28 to 2027-01-01 on 2 Apr 2026 (field history).

Products in scope (iContract, eProcurement, Merlin) and use cases (ANNA negotiation, Contract Intelligence, Guided Buying, Smart Invoices) were scoped in Oct 2025, but no RFP, formal evaluation, demo or SOW activity has followed.

metrics.narrative: Michael Katzeff articulated two concrete business pressures on the 31 Oct 2025 discovery call: a $1B+ hospital construction program and pharmaceutical procurement optimization, both hitting a team that has 'no dedicated procurement platform currently in place' as it migrates off PeopleSoft to Workday. That is real, substantial pain, but it was never quantified: no baseline spend under management, no manual-process cycle time, no tail-spend figure, no FTE burden and no target savings or ROI were stated. Mike McCarthy (Zycus) flagged that a business case for the CFO or board would be needed ('just about every deal we do requires a business case'), but with the buyer parked since October, no metrics have been co-developed. Quantifying the spend baseline and the manual-process cost is the metrics move once the buyer reactivates.
sources: Avoma discovery call 31 Oct 2025, Michael Katzeff
SF Next_Step__c interest summary (pharmaceutical procurement and $1B+ construction)

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified or engaged. The only Salesforce contact role is Michael Katzeff (Senior Manager of Procurement Operations, Influencer), who explicitly disclaimed budget authority: he said a decision 'is not something that we can just decide on our own because it involves other departments' and that it would route through the Contracting Director (Susan Wills), Legal and Finance, with a business case rolled up to 'your CFO or whoever has to get it, your board' (Avoma 31 Oct 2025). So the evidence shows the budget sits above Katzeff, in Finance/Contracting leadership, but no such person has been named in Salesforce, mapped, or contacted in ~248 days. Identifying and reaching the budget owner (Finance / Contracting leadership) is a top move; no name can be asserted because none exists in Salesforce.
sources: OpportunityContactRole (Michael Katzeff = Influencer, PRIMARY, only role)
Avoma discovery call 31 Oct 2025, Michael Katzeff

decision_criteria.narrative: Early criteria surfaced on the 31 Oct 2025 call are integration-led and adoption-led rather than a formal scored matrix. Katzeff probed the Workday integration directly ('you integrate into Workday and take over something like invoice scanning, matching, and matching to a purchase order') and both buyers valued streamlining across departments ('it will really help us streamline all our functions in the supply chain'). AI automation (ANNA autonomous negotiation, smart contracting, Smart Invoices, Teams-based intake) resonated as a differentiator. These are inferred interest signals, not confirmed evaluation criteria: no RFP, RFI, ShoeFit/BRD or use-case scoring round has run. Formal criteria will only firm up once the deal reactivates and a demo or evaluation is scoped.
sources: Avoma discovery call 31 Oct 2025, Michael Katzeff and Charles Maranga

decision_process.narrative: Katzeff described the process in his own words on 31 Oct 2025: procurement (himself and Charles Maranga) would first review Zycus materials, then loop in the Contracting team under Director Susan Wills 'because they're more familiar with a lot of our current agreements', with Legal and Finance/AP engaged as contract and invoicing scope widened, and a business case ultimately rolled to the CFO or board. Crucially he tied timing to existing vendor-contract renewal cycles and to the Workday go-live, saying the team would not move before the January stabilization. The process is therefore understood in outline but has never been executed: no stakeholder beyond procurement operations has been engaged, and the buyer stalled at the first step. The gating events (Workday live, contract-renewal timing) are the levers to restart it.
sources: Avoma discovery call 31 Oct 2025, Michael Katzeff

paper_process.narrative: Paper mechanics are unknown, appropriate to a still-early Qualified deal. Katzeff noted the account runs a distinct supply-chain Contracting team (separate from Legal) under Director Susan Wills that owns supplier agreements and renewal timelines, and that procurement decisions hinge on 'when those are up for renewal' (Avoma 31 Oct 2025). No MSA, Order Form, SOW, procurement channel, or InfoSec path has been discussed. The contract-expiration schedule held by the Contracting team is the natural forcing function; getting Susan Wills's team to share which current vendor agreements expire and when would create the first real paper-process visibility.
sources: Avoma discovery call 31 Oct 2025, Michael Katzeff

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is confirmed and named. Dana-Farber is migrating off PeopleSoft to Workday with 'no dedicated procurement platform currently in place' (Michael Katzeff, 31 Oct 2025), leaving requisitioning, approvals, contract management and invoice matching on manual or ERP-native processes precisely as a $1B+ hospital construction program and pharmaceutical procurement optimization load spike. Katzeff confirmed appetite to 'take some of the legwork out of some manual processes' with AI, and Charles Maranga agreed it 'will really help us streamline all our functions in the supply chain'. The pain owner is procurement operations (Katzeff/Maranga), with contract-side pain owned by the Contracting team. The pain is qualitative-confirmed but not yet quantified (see metrics).
sources: Avoma discovery call 31 Oct 2025, Michael Katzeff and Charles Maranga

champion.narrative: There is no champion. Michael Katzeff engaged well on the 31 Oct 2025 discovery call and gave useful process and stakeholder detail, but he has shown no champion behaviors: no introduction to the economic buyer or Contracting leadership, no internal advocacy, no competitive intelligence, and no proactive move to advance the deal. He asked for a SharePoint resource site and collateral to share internally, but there is no evidence any internal sharing or multi-threading followed. Decisively, he has not replied for ~8 months across two BDR re-engagement attempts (18 Mar and 19 Jun 2026). At best he was a coach on one call; the relationship is now dormant. Building a real champion (or replacing the entry point) is a prerequisite before any commercial step.
sources: Avoma discovery call 31 Oct 2025, Michael Katzeff
SF Next_Step__c re-engagement attempts 18 Mar 2026 and 19 Jun 2026 with no buyer reply

competition.narrative: No competitor is named in any source: Competitors__c is blank, no rival was mentioned on the 31 Oct 2025 call, and no task, next step or field-history entry references one. Katzeff described a greenfield posture ('no dedicated procurement platform currently in place') after the Workday migration, so there is no incumbent source-to-pay or CLM suite to displace, though the Contracting team does manage 'existing vendor contracts' whose scope is unknown. For this deal the real competition is inertia and the status quo, not a rival: the buyer parked the evaluation behind Workday and has stayed silent, so the dominant risk is a no-decision (they keep running procurement manually inside Workday) rather than a loss to Coupa, Ariba, GEP or Ivalua. 'No known competitor' here is a visibility state plus a do-nothing risk, not a confirmed sole-source win.
sources: Avoma discovery call 31 Oct 2025 (no competitor named)
SF Next_Step__c / tasks / field history (no competitive reference)

deal_movement.summary: No forward buyer movement since the 31 Oct 2025 discovery call. Everything since is rep-side: a close-date slip and repeated BDR re-engagement attempts, all unanswered. The deal has been effectively dormant for ~8 months behind a Workday go-live that has since cleared.

items.change: Discovery call held with Michael Katzeff and Charles Maranga; Zycus source-to-pay wheel walked through, SharePoint resource site offered
Buyer deferred evaluation until after the January Workday go-live
Short follow-up call (no transcript / not recorded)
BDR re-engagement attempt on Workday go-live progress, no buyer reply
Stage set to Qualified (from Initial Interest)
Second BDR re-engagement attempt on Workday go-live, no buyer reply

competitive_position.summary: No competitor is identified from any source (Competitors__c blank; none named on the 31 Oct 2025 call; nothing in tasks, next steps or field history). The account is greenfield post-Workday with no incumbent procurement suite to displace. The single strongest current threat is not a vendor at all: it is do-nothing / status-quo. Dana-Farber parked the evaluation behind the January Workday go-live and has stayed silent for ~8 months, so the live risk is a no-decision where procurement keeps running manually inside Workday. Treat 'no known competitor' as a visibility gap plus an inertia risk, not a sole-source lock.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / status quo (manual procurement inside Workday)
competitors.quote: I don't think that we would move forward before then... maybe it's something that we revisit again in New Year.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 31 Oct 2025, Michael Katzeff
competitors.how_we_win: Tie a scoped eProcurement + Smart Invoice quick win to the Workday go-live and the $1B construction spend spike, so automating now beats managing it manually; reach the Contracting team's renewal calendar as a forcing event.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: One discovery call established fit signals, not confirmed requirements: the buyer expects tight Workday integration, cross-department streamlining, and AI automation. All are aligned to Zycus strengths but none are validated by a demo or evaluation.

best_practice_check.summary: This is an early, dark deal single-threaded on one dormant Influencer. Winning requires restarting the relationship, widening beyond Katzeff, and finding a forcing event, before any commercial motion.
flags: Re-open the relationship: the account owner (not a BDR) should personally reach Michael Katzeff on the Workday go-live now that it has cleared, since two BDR attempts got no reply.
Break single-threading: engage Charles Maranga and Ronnie Crecco in parallel so the deal does not depend on one unresponsive contact.
Create a forcing event: get the Contracting team (Susan Wills) to share which current vendor agreements expire and when, turning contract-renewal timing into the evaluation trigger.

items.title: Unknown (Finance / Contracting leadership; CFO or board per Katzeff)
items.risk: Critical early-stage gap: Katzeff said approval requires higher-ups and a business case to the CFO or board, but no budget owner has been named in Salesforce or contacted in ~248 days. Without executive sponsorship the deal cannot leave discovery.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 31 Oct 2025, Michael Katzeff

champion_strength.summary: No champion. Michael Katzeff (Senior Manager of Procurement Operations, Influencer) was engaged and informative on the 31 Oct 2025 discovery call but has shown no advocacy: no executive or Contracting introduction, no internal selling, no competitive intelligence, and no move to advance the deal. He has been unresponsive for ~8 months across two BDR attempts (18 Mar, 19 Jun 2026). At best a one-call coach; the relationship is dormant and must be rebuilt or replaced.
champion_strength.source: Avoma discovery call 31 Oct 2025; OpportunityContactRole (Katzeff = Influencer, no Champion role); SF Next_Step__c re-engagement attempts 18 Mar and 19 Jun 2026 with no reply; last buyer contact 31 Oct 2025

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus led hard with AI on the 31 Oct 2025 call (Merlin control tower, ANNA autonomous negotiation agents, smart contracting, Smart Invoices, Teams-based intake) and it resonated, but nothing has been validated by a deeper demo. AI appetite is real yet parked; the Merlin/ANNA story needs to be put back in front of the buyer once they reactivate.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 6.0 / Moderate is consistent with the call: genuine interest in AI-driven automation (ANNA negotiation, Contract Intelligence, Guided Buying, Smart Invoices) but currently parked behind the Workday go-live and unvalidated by deeper demos. Not over- or under-rated versus the evidence.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is dark. One substantive discovery call (31 Oct 2025), one untranscribed follow-up (17 Feb 2026), then silence through two BDR re-engagement attempts. No buyer-initiated touch in ~8 months.
items: No buyer reply to BDR outreach on 18 Mar 2026 or 19 Jun 2026 (SF Next_Step__c).
Workday go-live blocker cleared ~5 months ago with no reactivation.

items.action: Justin emails Michael Katzeff directly on the cleared Workday go-live, proposing a scoped eProcurement demo.
items.trigger: Two BDR attempts (18 Mar, 19 Jun 2026) got no reply; Workday went live in January and is now stabilized.
items.expected_effect: Owner-level re-engagement is the only path to restart a dark deal; a scoped demo tied to the go-live can reopen the evaluation.

items.action: Multi-thread in parallel to Charles Maranga and Ronnie Crecco to break single-threading off one dormant contact.
items.trigger: Only contact role is Katzeff (Influencer), unresponsive ~8 months; Next Step names Maranga and Crecco as key contacts.
items.expected_effect: Widens coverage so the deal no longer depends on one silent Influencer and surfaces who now owns the initiative post-Workday.

items.action: Ask procurement to introduce the Contracting Director, Susan Wills, to surface vendor-contract expiry dates.
items.trigger: Katzeff said procurement decisions depend on when current vendor agreements are up for renewal, owned by the Contracting team.
items.expected_effect: Turns contract-renewal timing into a concrete forcing event and opens the CLM/Contract Intelligence use case with the right owner.

scope_change.detail: Product scope unchanged since Oct 2025; only the close date slipped (2026-05-28 to 2027-01-01). No modules dropped or amount cut.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A dark, early-stage discovery deal single-threaded on one Influencer who has ignored two re-engagement attempts; the real contest is do-nothing, and the only way forward is the owner reopening the relationship now that the Workday go-live has cleared.

win_position.text: We are barely in this: one discovery call on 31 Oct 2025, no economic buyer, no champion, and the sole contact Michael Katzeff has gone silent for ~8 months despite the Workday blocker clearing.

The fit is genuine and greenfield: Katzeff confirmed 'no dedicated procurement platform currently in place' and asked specifically about Zycus owning invoice matching into Workday (Avoma 31 Oct 2025).

The threat is inertia, not a rival: the buyer parked the evaluation ('I don't think that we would move forward before then', 31 Oct 2025) and never returned the contract-renewal timeline that would trigger it.

deal_momentum.text: Zero forward momentum: no stage progression in ~248 days and no buyer reply to BDR outreach on 18 Mar or 19 Jun 2026 (SF Next_Step__c).
The close date has already slipped from 2026-05-28 to 2027-01-01 (field history 2 Apr 2026) with no supporting buyer activity.

customer_commitment.text: Both buyers on the 31 Oct 2025 call engaged well and Charles Maranga said the platform 'will really help us streamline all our functions in the supply chain'.
But commitment stopped at collateral: Katzeff took a SharePoint resource site to share internally and there is no evidence any internal sharing, demo, or stakeholder expansion followed.

deal_risk.text: No economic buyer and no champion: approval requires higher-ups and a CFO/board business case that has never been built (Avoma 31 Oct 2025).
Single-threaded on one dormant Influencer; a reactivation could be led by Contracting or Finance with no Zycus relationship.

champion_strength.evidence: No champion; sole Influencer Katzeff dormant ~8 months across two BDR attempts.

buyer_engagement.evidence: No buyer-initiated touch since 31 Oct 2025; last_activity_date still 2025-10-31.

economic_buyer.evidence: No EB named in Salesforce or engaged; budget sits with Finance/Contracting leadership per Katzeff.

customer_preference.evidence: Positive but non-committal on one call ('it looks like a good tool'), then deferred everything to post-Workday and went silent; no Zycus-specific next step requested.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain confirmed ($1B+ construction, pharma procurement, no procurement platform) but no spend baseline, cycle time, or ROI quantified; Zycus flagged a CFO/board business case as still needed.

critical_signals.text: The last real buyer exchange was the 31 Oct 2025 discovery call; the buyer parked the evaluation behind the January Workday go-live and has not re-engaged since.
The Contracting Director Susan Wills and Senior Purchasing Specialist Ronnie Crecco are named but never met; the deal still rests on one dormant Influencer.
No rival is in play; this is greenfield with no procurement platform, so the real threat is a no-decision where they keep running procurement manually inside Workday.
No budget owner is engaged and no business case exists; approval would need a CFO or board case that has never been built.
